title: Piper pertinax
intro: Piper pertinax, also known as the rough pepper plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Piperaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of P. pertinax include piperine, a bioavailable compound that enhances the absorption of nutrients, as well as other alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, P. pertinax is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly consumed to promote digestive health and alleviate respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Piper pertinax is a species of plant in the family Piperaceae. Piperaceae is a family of flowering plants commonly known as pepper plants. This family is part of the order Piperales, which includes several other families of flowering plants. The order Piperales is within the clade magnoliids, a group of flowering plants thought to have originated around 140 million years ago. Piperaceae family is closely related to the family Aristolochiaceae.
